algorithms.title: Building and Escalating Surge Capacity
algorithms.description: Balancing the four S's and moving along the standards-of-care continuum.
steps: Expand across all four S's together — Staff, Stuff, Space, Systems — recognizing the weakest caps the surge.
Deliver conventional care with normal resources as long as possible.
Shift to contingency care with functionally equivalent adaptations (repurposed space, substituted supplies, adjusted ratios) as demand grows.
If demand overwhelms all adaptations, enter crisis standards of care by formal declaration under an ethical/legal framework.
Continuously address behavioral-health needs and CMIST functional needs.
De-escalate toward conventional care as resources recover; conduct after-action review.

algorithms.title: Psychological First Aid (PFA)
algorithms.description: The early, non-intrusive behavioral-health response for survivors and responders.
steps: Ensure physical safety and meet basic needs (shelter, food, water).
Provide a calm, reassuring presence; reduce arousal.
Connect people with loved ones, accurate information, and each other.
Support self-efficacy — help people take practical next steps.
Foster hope and link individuals to ongoing services.
Do NOT force survivors to recount the traumatic event.
Extend the same care to responders; monitor for cumulative stress and burnout.
